reviews.review_description: While playing it it all just felt janky including getting stuck in a hole and not being able to get out without a jump button while teleporting was bugged, the enemys becoming stiff after killing them, overall just a really buggy and janky game
Guys we paid nearly 30 quid and expect you to at least improve the mechanics and no response from devs DO NOT BUY THIS GAME

reviews.review_title: Have you ever wanted to be a samurai
reviews.review_description: This game will have you weapon samurai with different weapons worth the money

*there is a song in youtube called badlands from ELPHNT , if it were the main theme this game would feel more intense*

it has a little of games i really have enjoyed before, saints and sinners meets persona/ shin megami tensei with blade and sorcery fighting style in a monster hunter map esque, and it reminds me of shadow warrior aswell, hope it would have as many guns as it does with close range weapons

that drawing style in that town has somewhat of a depressive aura that reminds me of old shin megami tensei games, the soundtrack is mismatching a little bit with that, just my opinion, i feel like a slower "joy division" style of music fits this dreamy otherwordly place much more

not accounting for bugs overall i am happy with the purchase, hope the game gets updated and improved

As feedback i would sugest:

the first minutes feel a little bit slow and empty, maybe having just a house nearby where you spawn, some trees and such  would improve the first minutes impresion


if it were a little bit more "dark" like smt i would become a fan of this game, like having a more "demonic" soundtrack  as an option, having some more fearsome looking enemies and such


the artstyle is that of a japanese drawing i think, still if it had an option for more drawing distance it would be awesome (maybe for the meta quest 3)

having guns as a viable option from the begining (as much as swords) like shadow warrior style

more soundtrack options

Devs, you’ve definitely got a great idea here, but it’s not there yet. The physics don’t work at all and it’s buggy as hell. Found the game to be extremely frustrating for those reasons. It’s certainly not ready for the huge price tag. Perhaps a free version while it’s still in its early stages? Regardless, For some reason, I’m not able to get my money refunded. Please fix.￼

First, the art style is amazing it feels like youre actually in a manga, physics need a little polish but overall fairly good, when unsheathing the katana it actually slides, its not just a pop and its out, locations look pretty good, although the outside areas sometimes feels a bit empty, enemies are good but i think the animations are a bit fast, you can slice them in any axis, which is sick, not many games have it, weapons feel great most of the time, blood effects are also really good
Slicing does need to be improved a bit tho, it sometimes feels like a blunt weapon, stabbing feels great

Overall a 9/10 game, needs more attencion

This game is very fun. It has very fluid combat with a metroidvania gameplay loop. Teaming up with ally characters is really rewarding. The music is really original and catchy.  The art style is unique and beautiful.

This is also the best use of teleportation based movement I have ever seen. Using teleport to jump and dash is a unique solution to a  standard vr gameplay mechanic that normally bothers me. 

This game is early access so it has crashed on me twice, but thankfully I did not lose much progress.

This is a great game. It looks amazing. Cool music. Their is a story and quests. It definitely has minor bugs but it didn’t kill the gameplay. Hopefully the developer will get some help to fix this. This is definitely a cult classic in the making.
I'm wondering if you can do option please where we can turn the music down or off because it's too loud the music we need to listen to the characters

Needs an update and better physics.
And I can't find a map.
Nice idea with the masks.

reviews.review_title: Solid start at an insane price point
reviews.review_description:
- The combat feels alright; but all the other physics interactions do not. Especially sheathing your sword, ESPECIALLY. 
- I like the artstyle but the current execution looks like a mish mash of pure glitchiness in the town mainly. This is probably just a product of foveated rendering/reduced draw distance on the Quest, but you really can't help but notice it.
- The music is GREAT but it needs dynamics; walking through a barren empty field while listening to some crazy battle music, just kinda kills it.
- $30 is crazy for this title. I know there's a demo, but I would deeply suggest lowering the price a little, and putting it back up to the full 30 once the game seems complete and polished enough. I'm glad you at least provide a demo; but in it's current state, I still don't think this is worth the price.

I'll put my faith in and not refund this; let it marinate for a while and hope it grows into something truly great.

Biggest gripe is inconsistency with accessing 
my inventory or our little shoulder demon. It’s really aggravating to spend multiple minutes trying to find which point on my shoulder is going to work for me “this time”. Also, when facing multiple enemies my katana will get “caught” on things that make no sense, running around with 4 swords feeling almost like zoro is cool and all but reloading katanas just to avoid being disarmed by game mechanics is kinda crazy lol. Would love to see this fixed in an update. Maybe a smidge more character customization and these fixes and this is easily a 6 star game. Keep up the great work guys! Having fun with this one!

My weapons keep on getting stuck in the ground or in trees, but besides that it is a really good game. Could you add like more customization for your room so you could add like an actual bed or something, but keep up the good work.

This game is very fun there are some bugs, like the game crashing, but however the game has a few missions, very fun weapons and followers (and a dev that actually listens to the community) this game also perfectly fits the theme of  the game too.
